_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against Think C++ and the Google C++ Class outline. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: Classes & Objects
summary: Design types that keep related state and behavior together.
refresher: A class defines a new type. Private data members protect an object?s state, public methods provide controlled operations, and a constructor establishes a valid starting state. Each object owns its own member values.

blocks.body: A class lets you describe what a kind of object stores and what it can do. Instead of passing several related variables between functions, you give the relationship a name and keep the operations beside the data they protect.

Public members form the interface that other code may use. Private members are implementation details the class controls. A constructor runs when an object is created and should leave every member ready for the other methods.

blocks.caption: The constructor initializes the balance, deposit changes it through a rule, and the const getter reads it without modifying the object.
Public data lets any caller create an invalid state. A small public interface can enforce the class rule in one place.

blocks.body: Trace two Account objects separately. Depositing into the first changes only its balance member because each object has its own state. Methods know which object they are using through the implicit `this` pointer.

blocks.title: Initialize every member
blocks.body: A constructor body runs after data members have already been initialized. Prefer the member initializer list for their starting values, especially for const members and objects that have no default constructor.

blocks.prompt: What is the main job of a constructor?
blocks.why: The constructor initializes the new object so its public operations can rely on a valid state.

choices: It promises not to modify the object through that method
It makes the returned integer constant forever
blocks.why: The trailing const lets callers and the compiler know that reading the balance does not change the account.

blocks.title: Classes & Objects checklist
items: Give the class one clear responsibility.
Initialize every member in the constructor.
